food_name: Pizza with Cream Sauce and Mushroom

english: Pizza with Cream Sauce and Mushroom

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for cream mushroom pizza; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared pizza variants is limited.

main_ingredients: pizza dough (wheat flour, yeast, water, salt)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one slice of pizza weighing approximately 120 g, representing roughly one-sixth of a standard 25 cm Japanese-style cream mushroom pizza.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, cheese — HPB Singapore Foodo Database; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 21299 (Pizza with cheese, regular crust) for saturated fat estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record for Pizza Cream Mushroom (cr_id G00010741, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cheese pizza (regular crust) at approximately 3.2 g/100 g, adjusted upward to 3.8 g/100 g to reflect the heavier cream sauce characteristic of Japanese-style cream mushroom pizza. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrients not reported in HPB source; set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on pizza (regular crust, cheese) GI ~60
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Japanese-style cream mushroom pizza. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on regular-crust cheese pizza (GI ~60, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). The cream sauce and mushroom topping do not substantially alter the GI relative to the dough base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (60) × available carbohydrate per serving (120 g × 0.3238 = 38.86 g) / 100 = 23.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (15.75 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1.7 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; cream sauce contributes saturated fat promoting arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit intake to one slice, request a reduced-cream or olive-oil-based sauce to lower saturated fat, and pair with a high-fibre side salad to blunt the glycaemic and sodium-retention response.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat dough delivers 32.4 g/100 g carbohydrate with a GI of approximately 60; sugar at 15.75 g/100 g with only 1.7 g/100 g dietary fibre predicts a rapid postprandial glucose spike
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should restrict to one small slice, request a wholemeal or cauliflower crust to reduce glycaemic load, retain mushroom topping for its beta-glucan benefit, and monitor postprandial blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat of approximately 3.8 g/100 g from cream sauce and cheese; high sugar (15.75 g/100 g) contributes to dyslipidaemia risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion to one slice, substitute heavy cream sauce with a lighter tomato or olive-oil base to reduce saturated fat intake, and avoid frequent consumption given the combined saturated fat and high-sugar profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pizza with Cream Sauce and Mushroom is a Japanese-style Western-fusion pizza featuring a hand-stretched, fermented wheat dough base topped with a rich cream sauce (heavy cream and butter), sliced mushrooms, and melted mozzarella cheese. The dough is prepared from scratch through mixing, fermentation, and hand-stretching, yielding a chewy, slightly airy crust. The cream sauce imparts a rich, savoury, mildly sweet flavour profile, while mushrooms contribute umami depth and a soft, earthy texture. This style of pizza is popular in Japanese casual dining and café chains, where Western pizza formats are adapted with cream-based sauces and local ingredient preferences. Per 100 g, the pizza provides 204 kcal, 32.4 g carbohydrate, 7.5 g fat (estimated 3.8 g saturated), 1.84 g protein, 15.75 g sugar, 1.7 g dietary fibre, and 130 mg sodium. The high sugar content relative to fibre raises concern for blood glucose management and insulin-mediated sodium retention. The cream sauce elevates saturated fat, which is relevant for cardiovascular and inflammatory conditions. Mushrooms provide beta-glucans with modest immunomodulatory and glycaemic-buffering benefits. Clinically, this pizza is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis without significant portion control and ingredient modification.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of cream mushroom pizza?
Can diabetics eat cream mushroom pizza?
Is Japanese cream pizza high in sugar?
What is the GI of cream mushroom pizza?
Is cream mushroom pizza suitable for hypertension?
How much sodium is in cream mushroom pizza?
What is クリームマッシュルームピザ?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record (cr_id G00010741, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheese pizza entry with documented upward adjustment for cream sauce. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (regular-crust cheese pizza, GI ~60) with documented basis.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, fat-soluble vitamins, B-vitamins, and moisture data for this Japanese-style cream mushroom pizza are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat estimated at 3.8 g/100 g based on USDA SR Legacy entry 21299 (cheese pizza, regular crust) adjusted upward for heavier cream sauce; this is an approximation and should be verified against laboratory analysis if available. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (regular-crust cheese pizza); no direct GI measurement for Japanese cream mushroom pizza was found in approved sources. The input record classified primary_cuisine as Japanese, which is consistent with the Japanese café-style cream pizza format sold in Singapore; multilingual naming requirement for Japanese dishes is satisfied with クリームマッシュルームピザ. No Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi names are required as this is classified as a Japanese dish, not a Singaporean dish. Halal status is unconfirmed as cream and cheese sourcing is not specified.

improvement_actions_taken: Renamed food_name from promotional menu string 'Pizza Cream Mushroom' to canonical English 'Pizza with Cream Sauce and Mushroom'.
Added Japanese local name クリームマッシュルームピザ and romanized form Kuriimu Masshurūmu Piza to satisfy G6 Japanese cuisine multilingual requirement.
Added saturated_fat_g (3.8 g/100 g) estimated from USDA SR Legacy entry 21299 with documented upward adjustment for cream sauce.
Added GI of 60 (medium) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, with glycemic_load_per_serving calculated at 23.3 g.

Replaced input health_suitability conditions (diabetes, hypertension, rheumatoid_arthritis) with RESULT template health_context structure including required Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes (machine key type2_diabetes), and Cardiovascular Disease conditions.
Set all unavailable nutrient fields to -1 and documented each in notes with reason and source consulted.
Added FoodOn URI, FoodEx2 code, and SNOMED CT gap note to ontology section.
Populated aliases_by_language and verified all entries present verbatim in flat aliases array.
Added serving size (120 g, 1 slice) with natural language portion description.
Added llm_training section with 1,247-character natural language description, common user queries, meal context, and ingredient synonyms.
